note: seq=138: smarter autopull heal script — only START the applier when it is IDLE and stale (>5min); only FORCE-RESTART a running applier if it is clearly hung (>15min no cursor progress). Prevents the watchdog from killing a legitimately-running (just slow) applier mid-drain. Writes /opt/atlas/autopull_heal.sh (allowed path); no run step.

steps.content:
#!/bin/bash
f=/var/lib/atlas/autopull/last_seq
t=$(stat -c %Y "$f" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); now=$(date +%s); age=$((now-t))
if systemctl is-active --quiet atlas-autopull.service; then
  if [ $age -gt 900 ]; then systemctl reset-failed atlas-autopull.service 2>/dev/null; systemctl restart --no-block atlas-autopull.service; fi
else
  if [ $age -gt 300 ]; then systemctl reset-failed atlas-autopull.service 2>/dev/null; systemctl start --no-block atlas-autopull.service; fi
fi
systemctl is-active --quiet atlas-autopull.timer || systemctl restart atlas-autopull.timer
systemctl is-active --quiet atlas-autopull-heal.timer || systemctl restart atlas-autopull-heal.timer
